pairs.question: Who were the founding members of D.R.I. and when was the band formed?
pairs.answer: D.R.I. was formed in 1982 by Kurt Brecht (lead vocals), Spike Cassidy (guitar and backing vocals), Dennis Johnson (bass), and Eric Brecht (drums).

pairs.question: What was D.R.I.'s first album released under Metal Blade Records, and in what year did it come out?
pairs.answer: D.R.I.'s first album released under Metal Blade Records was their self-titled debut album, which came out in 1983.

pairs.question: How many studio albums has D.R.I. released to date?
pairs.answer: D.R.I. has released seven studio albums to date.

pairs.question: Can you explain the significance of the album '4 of a Kind' in D.R.I.'s discography?
pairs.answer: The album '4 of a Kind' was significant because it was D.R.I.'s first album to chart on the Billboard 200, reaching No. 116. Released in 1988, this milestone marked a peak in their commercial success during the thrash metal era.

pairs.question: What was D.R.I.'s last studio album to chart in the US, and when was it released?
pairs.answer: D.R.I.'s last studio album to chart in the US was 'Thrash Zone,' which was released in 1987 and peaked at No. 140 on the American charts.

pairs.question: Has D.R.I. released any new music after 1995? If so, provide details.
pairs.answer: Yes, D.R.I. has released new music after 1995. In 2004, they released a demo track titled 'Against Me' as a digital download on their website. Additionally, in 2016, they released an EP titled 'But Wait... There's More!' containing new music.

pairs.question: What is the current lineup of D.R.I. as of the latest available information?
pairs.answer: As of the latest available information, the current lineup of D.R.I. consists of Kurt Brecht (lead vocals), Spike Cassidy (guitar and backing vocals), Harald Oimoen (bass), and Rob Rampy (drums).
